MINI MINUTES FOR DEC-28-2015 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
ORDER DENYING MOTION TO SET ASIDE SUMMARY JUDGMENT

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT FINANCIAL CASUALTY & SURETY, INC'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE SUMMARY JUDGMENT, DISCHARGE FORFEITURE, AND EXONERATE BAIL IS DENIED. ALTHOUGH THE LAW TRADITIONALLY DISFAVORS FORFEITURES, INCLUDING FORFEITURES OF BAIL, FINANCIAL CASUALTY & SURETY, INC. HAS FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT IT IS ENTITLED TO RELIEF UNDER CCP SECTION 473(B) OR (D). (SEE PEOPLE V. UNITED BONDING INS. CO. (1971) 5 CAL.3D 898.) NOTABLY, THE CASES FINANCIAL CASUALTY & SURETY, INC. RELIES UPON IN ORDER TO SUPPORT ITS REQUEST FOR RELIEF INVOLVE CCP SECTION 473(B). (SEE PEOPLE V. NORTH RIVER INS. CO. (2011) 200 CAL.APP.4TH 712 AND COUNTY OF LOS ANGELES V. FINANCIAL CASUALTY & SURETY INC. (2015) 236 CAL.APP.4TH 37.) FINANCIAL CASUALTY & SURETY, INC. HAS NOT SUBMITTED ARGUMENT OR EVIDENCE WHICH DEMONSTRATE THAT ITS INITIAL MOTION TO VACATE FORFEITURE WAS DENIED DUE TO ITS MISTAKE, INADVERTENCE, SURPRISE, OR EXCUSABLE NEGLECT. BECAUSE PLAINTIFF COMPLIED WITH PENAL CODE SECTIONS 1305 AND 1306 IN MOVING FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, THE JUDGMENT IS NOT VOIDABLE UNDER CCP SECTION 473(D). ADDITIONALLY, FINANCIAL CASUALTY & SURETY, INC. HAS NOT PRESENTED ANY EVIDENCE TO SUGGEST THAT THERE WAS A CLERICAL ERROR WHICH WOULD WARRANT RELIEF UNDER CCP SECTION 473(D.) BECAUSE FINANCIAL CASUALTY & SURETY, INC. HAS FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT IT IS ENTITLED TO RELIEF UNDER CCP SECTION 473, THE MOTION IS DENIED. THE COURT GRANTED MR. WHITE'S REQUEST TO STAY PAYMENT OF BOND UNDER PENAL CODE SECTION 1308 FOR 5-DAYS FOR FILING OF AN APPEAL (LANGUAGE INCLUDED IN THE ORDER). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489. (302/EG)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Summary Judgement
Off Calendar Dec-28-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Diana Rosentein of Office of The City Attorney telephone number 415-554-3933 for Plaintiff.
Robert White of Attorneys at Law telephone number 714-617-9600 appeared by telephone for Defendant Financial Casualty & Surety.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT FINANCIAL CASUALTY & SURETY, INC'S Motion To Set Aside Summary Judgment, Discharge Forfeiture, and Exonerate Bail is denied. Although the law traditionally disfavors forfeitures, including forfeitures of bail, Financial Casualty & Surety, Inc. has failed to demonstrate that it is entitled to relief under CCP section 473(b) or (d). (See People v. United Bonding Ins. Co. (1971) 5 Cal.3d 898.) Notably, the cases Financial Casualty & Surety, Inc. relies upon in order to support its request for relief involve CCP section 473(b). (See People v. North River Ins. Co. (2011) 200 Cal.App.4th 712 and County of Los Angeles v. Financial Casualty & Surety Inc. (2015) 236 Cal.App.4th 37.) Financial Casualty & Surety, Inc. has not submitted argument or evidence which demonstrate that its initial motion to vacate forfeiture was denied due to its mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or excusable neglect. Because Plaintiff complied with Penal Code sections 1305 and 1306 in moving for summary judgment, the judgment is not voidable under CCP section 473(d). Additionally, Financial Casualty & Surety, Inc. has not presented any evidence to suggest that there was a clerical error which would warrant relief under CCP section 473(d.) Because Financial Casualty & Surety, Inc. has failed to demonstrate that it is entitled to relief under CCP section 473, the motion is denied. The Court granted Mr. White's request to stay payment of bond under Penal Code Section 1308 for 5-days for filing of an appeal (language included in the order). Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489. (302/EG)
